description: Following Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter in late 2022, the platform transitioned from a legacy verification system for public figures to a paid subscription model for blue checkmarks. The change sparked intense debate regarding platform credibility, impersonation risks, and the democratization of digital status symbols.

key_events.description: Announcement of the $8 subscription plan for legacy verified status.
Widespread impersonation of corporations like Eli Lilly causing stock market volatility.
Twitter temporarily pauses sign-ups for Twitter Blue due to massive abuse of the system.
Final removal of legacy blue checkmarks, cementing the pay-to-play model.

calibration_notes: This scenario highlights the tension between monetization goals and platform integrity, providing a model for how rapid policy shifts in social media can lead to immediate public backlash and volatility in user trust.
